specification.topic-statement: The agent tree navigation system switches the active agent by traversing parent-child-sibling relationships within the in-memory agent array.

scope.in-scope: Switching the current agent, navigating to previous or next sibling, navigating to a parent agent, navigating to the most recently created child agent, and updating the navigation display context in the separator layer.
scope.boundary: This topic reads agent records (uuid, parent_uuid, created_at) from the array and updates the current pointer. It does not add or remove agents from the array.
This topic sends navigation context (prev sibling UUID, next sibling UUID, current UUID, child count) to the separator layer for display. Rendering of that context is handled externally.

behavior.switching-active-agent: When a switch is requested with a null target, an invalid-argument error is returned. When the target equals the current agent, the operation returns success immediately without any state change. Otherwise, the current agent pointer is updated to the target and the navigation display context is refreshed.
behavior.navigating-to-previous-sibling:
Siblings are all agents whose parent identifier matches the current agent's parent identifier. Two null parent identifiers are considered equal (both root-level agents are siblings of each other).
All sibling agents are collected into a fixed-capacity local buffer of 64 slots by scanning the full agent array.
If more than 64 agents share the same parent, writes past the end of the 64-slot buffer occur. No bounds check exists on the collection loop. This is a silent memory corruption hazard.
If one or fewer siblings are found, the operation returns success immediately with no state change. Otherwise, the current agent's position within the sibling list is located, and the previous sibling is selected with wraparound: if the current agent is first in the list, the last sibling is selected. The switch is then performed.
behavior.navigating-to-next-sibling: Uses the same sibling collection algorithm as previous-sibling navigation. Navigation advances forward by one position modulo the sibling count, wrapping from last back to first.
behavior.navigating-to-parent:
If the current agent has no parent identifier (it is a root agent), the operation returns success immediately with no state change.
Otherwise, the parent is looked up in the agent array by exact UUID match. If the parent is not found (it was removed from the array after being killed or reaped), the operation returns success immediately with no state change. If found, the switch is performed.
behavior.navigating-to-most-recent-child: The agent array is scanned for agents whose parent identifier equals the current agent's UUID. Among matching agents, the one with the highest created_at timestamp is selected. If multiple children have identical timestamps (including the legacy value of zero), the first one encountered in array order wins. If no children are found, the operation returns success with no state change. Otherwise, the switch is performed.
behavior.updating-navigation-display-context:
This operation is triggered automatically after every agent switch. It is also called after fork and kill operations.
If the current agent is null or the separator layer is absent, the operation returns immediately without updating anything.
The full agent array is scanned once to compute: - Previous sibling: the sibling with the highest created_at value that is strictly less than the current agent's created_at. - Next sibling: the sibling with the lowest created_at value that is strictly greater than the current agent's created_at. - Child count: the number of agents whose parent identifier matches the current agent's UUID.
Sibling comparison uses the same parent-equality logic as navigation (null parent identifiers are equal). The resulting UUIDs (which may be null for prev and next) and the child count are sent to the separator layer.
